items.headline: Nine new homes approved in subdivision near Old Trace Road
items.summary: The City Council approved a plan to divide a vacant 1-acre lot into nine single-family homes and seven junior accessory dwelling units (JADUs) on Old Trace Road. This is one of the larger residential developments recently approved in Palo Alto's Residential Estate zone.

items.headline: City approves lease bonds for new public safety building
items.summary: Mountain View City Council approved lease revenue bonds to finance a new public safety building project on April 28. This represents a significant capital investment in police and fire facilities for the community.

items.headline: Council reviews draft biodiversity and urban forest plan
items.summary: The City Council held a study session on April 28 to review a draft Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan. This plan will guide how Mountain View manages trees and natural habitat in the city going forward.

items.headline: Zingsters restaurant opens at 220 University Avenue
items.summary: A new restaurant called Zingsters is setting up signage in downtown Palo Alto's commercial district. This represents a new dining establishment in the city's main business area.

items.headline: Mixed-use development proposed with 67 homes and retail space
items.summary: A 4.22-acre Sunnyvale site could be redeveloped with 55 townhomes, 12 apartments, and over 18,500 square feet of retail including a new two-story building, while keeping the existing Speedy's Tacos location. The project would be subdivided into 17 residential lots plus common areas.

items.headline: New Dental Office Opens at The Villages
items.summary: Bay Hill Dentistry is opening a new dental office at 2933 The Villages Parkway ($629k project). This adds healthcare services to the popular residential community on the city's south side.

items.headline: Campbell revises business license tax rates
items.summary: The City Council is considering an ordinance to update business license tax rates in Campbell's municipal code. The change takes effect in the next fiscal year and may affect local business costs.

items.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: Santa Clara approves massive stadium signage
items.summary: The City Council authorized large supergraphic signs on building surfaces near Levi's Stadium during FIFA World Cup 2026 events. This represents a temporary visual transformation of the stadium area to support the international soccer tournament coming to Santa Clara.
